channel.title: The Grant Desk - EU, added in last 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: EU, added in last 30 days.

item.title: FOMAS Group Special Prize 2026 (within Arte Laguna Prize)
item.description:
Organisation: FOMAS Group (in collaboration with Arte Laguna Prize)

Location: Italy (FOMAS HQ Lecco; presented within Arte Laguna at Arsenale Nord, Venice)

Award: First prize EUR 7,000; second EUR 4,000; third EUR 3,000 (all taxes included). NOTE: works are entered via the Arte Laguna Prize, which has a registration fee (approx. EUR 97-122). The winning works become FOMAS Group property (rights of economic use, reproduction, communication to the public, exhibition, distribution and digitisation transferred to FOMAS).

Duration: One-off special prize awarded within Arte Laguna Prize 2026

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Special Prize from FOMAS Group, an Italian multinational in forging/rolling/metal powders, awarded within the Arte Laguna Prize 2026. THEME: 'the journey of steel in its crossing from hot to cold' - a metamorphosis of matter from incandescent casting to a cooled, smooth, precise surface; works should evoke this transition. FORMAT: hangable panel or canvas, dimensions between 1.5x1.5 m (minimum) and 3x2 m (maximum); unconventional materials encouraged. THREE AWARDS to nominated works: EUR 7,000 first, EUR 4,000 second, EUR 3,000 third (taxes included), paid after the work is presented. IMPORTANT CONDITION: the winning works become the property of FOMAS Group, with the artists ceding ownership and rights of economic use, reproduction, communication to the public, exhibition, distribution and digitisation for institutional and promotional purposes. APPLY by submitting via the Arte Laguna Prize portal (which has a registration fee, approx. EUR 97-122 for the full prize). Registration for the FOMAS Special Award closes 1 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Malakta x Kulturhuset Dux: Community Art Commission and Residency 2026 (Kristinestad)
item.description:
Organisation: Art in Malakta rf (with Kulturhuset Dux and Kristinestad Artist Residency)

Location: Kristinestad / Malax, Finland

Award: EUR 6,000 artist fee plus EUR 5,000 materials budget, with travel and residency accommodation included. No application fee.

Duration: One-month residency 1 Sep - 4 Oct 2026 (Phase 1) plus production phase, with artwork completed by summer 2027 (Phase 2)

Deadline: Closed 31 May 2026

Commission and residency from Art in Malakta rf with Kulturhuset Dux and Kristinestad Artist Residency to create a public artwork for Kulturhuset Dux or its immediate surroundings, in dialogue with Kristinestad and its residents. The medium is open (installation, object, sound, textile, light, digital); what matters is the relationship between the work, the site and the people. The project is rooted in Kristinestad's identity as a Cittaslow town and as Baltic Sea Cultural Pearl 2026. PHASE 1 (1 Sep - 4 Oct 2026): a one-month residency at Kristinestad Artist Residency for research, meetings, workshops, and artistic development. PHASE 2: production after the residency (remotely or at Malakta if needed); the artwork must be completed by summer 2027 and requires on-site presence. ELIGIBILITY: open to artists or artist collectives wanting to work site-specifically and in dialogue with residents. APPLY by email to communityart@malakta.fi by 31 May 2026 with a motivation letter, portfolio, CV and website link as a single PDF (max 5 MB). Interviews before 19 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: SOFTER: Cyberdeck Residency 2026 (Copenhagen)
item.description:
Organisation: SOFTER

Location: SOFTER Lab, Copenhagen, Denmark

Award: Per person and incl. VAT: EUR 2,500 participation fee, plus EUR 1,000 materials budget, EUR 100 lunch, and EUR 1,250 travel and accommodation support (for participants not based in Copenhagen). No application fee.

Duration: Two-week residency, 3-16 August 2026; tutorial deliverable by 1 September 2026

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Two-week residency at SOFTER Lab in Copenhagen inviting creative technologists and curious makers to design, build and demystify cyberdecks (custom-built portable computers typically based on single-board computers like a Raspberry Pi, with a homemade case reflecting the builder's aesthetic). The residency frames cyberdecks as a way to reclaim agency and creativity within tech, bridging the DIY hacker community and creative technologists. Selected residents source materials (with a materials budget and pointers to local electronic recycling), build their own cyberdeck, document the process, and produce a beginner-friendly 10-20 minute video tutorial (material list, tools, any 3D-printing files) ready for publication on 1 September 2026. An optional show-and-tell workshop or talk can be hosted at SOFTER Lab or the SOFTER Digital Futures conference (1-3 October 2026). Facilities include a workspace, GoPro for documentation, 3D printer and filament, basic soldering, and design/graphics support, with three SOFTER check-ins (kick-off, mid-process, evaluation). WHO: a team of two (apply as a pair on one project, or two individuals working alongside each other); backgrounds from curious beginner to hardware expert; interest in hardware/teaching/documentation. Applicants are selected on idea originality, functionality, purpose and design. Deadline 1 June 2026, 12:00 CEST; decisions within two weeks. Contact: ida@softer.global.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: transmediale 2027: Lattice Labs Open Call
item.description:
Organisation: transmediale

Location: Berlin, Germany (in-person sprint and festival) plus online sessions

Award: Not paid (no artist fee or stipend). transmediale covers travel costs to Berlin (train prioritised; otherwise round-trip economy flights) for each participant to attend the festival and the design sprint, plus accommodation for the duration of both. Letters of invitation provided for visa applications. No application fee.

Duration: Multi-block programme: online vocabulary sessions, a 3-day Berlin design sprint (October 2026), online implementation/festival prep (Nov-Dec 2026), and presentation at transmediale festival (January 2027)

Deadline: 07 Jun 2026

transmediale 2027's Lattice Labs are experimental working groups dedicated to rethinking and testing alternatives to technological monocultures of extraction and surveillance, in preparation of the festival. Each Lab has roughly five participants guided by an experienced artist or technologist, structured around flat hierarchies and peer-to-peer learning, with the aim of producing prototypes, tools and projects that articulate shared languages to organise, resist and live otherwise. Programme blocks include online Collective Vocabulary Sessions (Block 1), a 3-day in-person design sprint in Berlin (Block 2, October 2026), online implementation and festival prep (Block 3, November-December 2026), a workshop/panel presentation at transmediale (Block 4, January 2027), and ongoing long-term organising afterwards (Block 5). WHO: early-career and emerging artists, researchers, curators, students and practitioners from any discipline (art and culture, design, tech, writing, publishing, academia, law, labour organising, etc.); transdisciplinary cohorts are sought. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be based in (and around) the EU. Only one application per candidate across all labs; no collective applications. APPLY via the transmediale submission form by 7 June 2026 (23:59 CEST); participants announced 29 June 2026. Contact: submit[at]transmediale.de.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Talos Network: AI Policy Leaders Programme 2026 (Autumn cohort)
item.description:
Organisation: Talos Network

Location: Europe (virtual bootcamp + week-long Brussels summit; placements in Brussels or other European cities)

Award: Placement track: a 12-month paid placement at an AI-focused organisation, base EUR 5,000/month for Brussels (adjusted for cost of living elsewhere). Plus a fully-reimbursed 6-day Brussels policymaking summit, tailored financial support (coworking, conference attendance, career-transition funding), and intensive 1-1 mentoring. No application fee.

Duration: Virtual bootcamp 10 Sep - 22 Oct 2026 (approx. 10 hrs/week) plus a 12-month paid placement (placement track)

Deadline: 14 Jun 2026

The Talos Network's Policy Leaders Programme is built for experienced professionals ready to move into shaping European governance of advanced AI. It runs a part-time virtual European AI Policy Bootcamp (10 Sep - 22 Oct 2026, weekly Thursday calls, ~10 hrs/week, designed around a full-time job) with a fully-reimbursed week-long policymaking summit in Brussels (3-9 Oct 2026), and a Placement track offering a 12-month paid placement at a relevant AI-focused organisation (base EUR 5,000/month for Brussels). Fellows get intensive 1-1 mentoring and career coaching, access to the Talos Network and alumni, a curated senior peer cohort, and tailored financial support. ELIGIBILITY: experienced professionals (around 8-10+ years) in high-stakes environments such as government, international organisations, think tanks, industry or academia, with a track record of influencing senior decision-makers, looking to transition into AI governance or strengthen the AI dimension of their existing work; Europe-focused. Application deadline 14 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Realities in Transition: RiT 2 Open Call #2 - Telepresence in the Digital Space
item.description:
Organisation: Realities in Transition, in collaboration with MEET Digital Culture Center Milan

Location: Hybrid: online phases plus a two-month in-person period at MEET Digital Culture Center, Milan, Italy

Award: Three grants of EUR 15,000 each (one per artist), covering the artist's fee plus travel, accommodation and subsistence, disbursed in three installments (EUR 7,000 on signing, EUR 4,000 after the Test Lab, EUR 4,000 at the end). Production costs of up to EUR 5,000 are covered by the host, plus a mentoring programme. No application fee.

Duration: Six-month hybrid residency (online October-December 2026; in-person at MEET Milan January-February 2027; online February-March 2027), final project presented June 2027

Deadline: 10 Jun 2026

Open call from the Realities in Transition (RiT 2 - Unwritten Worlds) programme for artists and designers to co-create a collective XR artwork during a residency exploring telepresence and the shifting nature of presence in the digital age. The residency investigates how the body operates across physical and digital dimensions through motion capture and immersive environments, treating movement as a language that can be encoded, transformed and transmitted, and opening critical and speculative perspectives on authorship, identity and corporeality. MEDIUM: motion capture and immersivity. ELIGIBILITY: individual XR and immersive artists who are European residents; three artists are selected and joined by a creative technologist to work as a group. The residency alternates shared work, critical discussion and experimentation, from conception and prototyping to a public Test Lab and audience presentation, with a mentoring programme covering XR mediation, ethics and inclusion, accessibility, hybridisation of spaces, distribution and business models. Apply via the Realities in Transition open-call page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Hubert Butler Essay Prize 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Hubert Butler Essay Prize (with Haus Publishing)

Location: Ireland (online submission)

Award: EUR 2,500 prize (sponsored by Haus Publishing). No entry fee.

Duration: Annual essay prize

Deadline: 03 Jul 2026

Annual essay prize for a previously unpublished essay of up to 3,000 words on a set theme. The 2026 theme takes Auden's line 'Poetry makes nothing happen' as a prompt about what impact high culture can have in a world facing crisis - well suited to a critical essayist working on AI, media and society. Judged blind, so it is open to and fair for debut writers (no publication history required). ELIGIBILITY: entrants must be over 18 and a citizen of the UK or an EU member state. Deadline 3 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Stinging Fly: Open Submissions (Creative Nonfiction)
item.description:
Organisation: The Stinging Fly

Location: Dublin, Ireland (international submissions)

Award: Paid on acceptance: nonfiction approx. EUR 50/page (minimum EUR 375, maximum EUR 1,250). No submission fee.

Duration: Per piece; submissions accepted in timed windows

Deadline: 23 Nov 2026

Leading Irish literary magazine that publishes and pays for creative nonfiction (as well as fiction and poetry), with a stated particular interest in promoting new and emerging writers. A clean, no-fee, internationally-open outlet for a standalone critical/creative-nonfiction piece. ELIGIBILITY: Irish and international writers; debut-friendly, no published-book requirement. Submissions are accepted in specific windows; the next nonfiction window runs Monday 9 November to Monday 23 November 2026 (closes 5pm Irish time) for the Summer 2027 issue.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: FPP Residency & Exhibition 2026 (Konstkollektivet / Flower Power Photography)
item.description:
Organisation: Konstkollektivet, with Flower Power Photography, Centrum for fotografi and Artist in Coexistence (AiC)

Location: Orust and Molndal, Sweden

Award: Artist fee 7,000 SEK plus exhibition fee 3,400 SEK, travel support up to 4,000 SEK (slow travel by train/bus encouraged), and accommodation for the whole period. Plus facilitation, mentorship and research support. No application fee. (Amounts in SEK, incl. VAT where applicable.)

Duration: 14-day residency 3-17 August 2026; group exhibition opens 1 September 2026

Deadline: 14 Jun 2026

Residency and exhibition for visual artists whose practice engages with experimental, alternative and/or critical image-making (analogue processes, archives or hybrid approaches), with a particular interest in image-making as a way of living and thinking alongside the planet and other beings, embracing slowness, collaboration and more-than-human care. Organised by Konstkollektivet with Flower Power Photography (an artistic research project by Sam McCarthy and Lasse Lindqvist on non-violence, ecology and more-than-human ethics), Centrum for fotografi, and Artist in Coexistence (AiC). Includes a 14-day residency on the island of Orust (3-15 August), a process presentation, a workshop contribution at Konstkollektivet (15-16 August), and participation in a group exhibition at Konstparken opening 1 September, plus accommodation, an artist fee (7,000 SEK), exhibition fee (3,400 SEK), and travel support up to 4,000 SEK. ELIGIBILITY: European-based visual artists of all ages and nationalities. APPLICATION: one PDF (max 10MB) with a project proposal/motivation (max 1 page), artist statement (max 150 words), CV (1 page) and portfolio (max 5 pages) to residens@konstkollektivet.se, subject line 'Name_LastName_FPP_Residency', by 14 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Prototype Fund Class 03 (Open-Source Software)
item.description:
Organisation: Prototype Fund (Open Knowledge Foundation Germany)

Location: Germany / EU (lead must be based in Germany; funded members must reside in the EU)

Award: Up to EUR 95,000 over 6 months, or up to EUR 158,000 over 10 months, plus coaching. No application fee.

Duration: 6 or 10 months

Deadline: 30 Nov 2026

Prototype Fund supports the development of socially relevant open-source software, offering low-threshold funding and coaching to individual developers and small teams. ELIGIBILITY: individual developers or teams of up to four; funded members must reside in the EU and the lead entity must be based in Germany. Application window 1 October to 30 November 2026. Apply via the Prototype Fund application page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Jan Michalski Foundation: Writers Residency 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Jan Michalski Foundation for Writing and Literature

Location: Montricher, Switzerland (Swiss Jura, near Lausanne and Geneva)

Award: Weekly allowance of CHF 400, plus travel costs to and from home covered, accommodation in a private cabin, and breakfast and lunch provided. Electric bikes and library access included.

Duration: Two weeks to three months (first 2027 residents arrive mid-January 2027)

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

Residency for writers and translators at the Jan Michalski Foundation in Montricher, Switzerland, set in seven distinctive 'cabin' modules hanging from an openwork canopy, offering conditions to start, continue or finish a writing project. Around forty authors from around the world (emerging to established) are hosted each year. Open to all kinds of writing and all languages, with priority given to literary writers and translators; other disciplines are welcome as long as literature is at the heart of the project. Residencies are for individuals or pairs working on a common project (e.g. a writer and a translator). A percentage of residencies are dedicated to nature writing. WHAT IS PROVIDED: a private cabin for independent living (a separate cabin per person for pairs; one accessible cabin for reduced mobility), travel costs to and from home, a weekly allowance of CHF 400, breakfast and lunch, electric bikes, and daytime library access. ELIGIBILITY: no age or nationality restrictions; beginners accepted; one application per year; former residents may not reapply (families, children and pets cannot be accommodated). Stays cannot be split into multiple periods. Selection is by a panel chaired by Vera Michalski-Hoffmann, assessing the literary quality of the project, the candidate's background, and whether the stay length matches the project scope. APPLICATION: online in English or French (work excerpts may be in any language); the 2027 form is open 2 June to 31 August 2026; selected candidates announced December 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: LPM 2026 Rome: Call to Participate (Live Performers Meeting)
item.description:
Organisation: LPM - Live Performers Meeting

Location: Rome, Italy

Award: Paid open call, not a grant: participation requires an artist/guest subscription of EUR 20 (includes a pass for all activities, a T-shirt, a daily drink, and a 30-minute performance slot), plus 20% off workshops. The Live Immersive Contest offers prizes from technical partners.

Duration: Festival edition in Rome 2026

Deadline: 16 Aug 2026

Open call to participate in LPM (Live Performers Meeting), described as the biggest worldwide event dedicated to audiovisual performing arts, returning to Rome for its 27th edition. Artists can take part with AV performances, VJ sets and live mapping (a 30-minute slot to perform); workshops, lectures and project showcases; interactive AV installations; and the Live Immersive Contest (competing for prizes from technical partners). NOTE: this is a participation call rather than a funding opportunity - it requires a paid subscription (EUR 20 as artist or guest) which includes a festival pass, a 30-minute slot, a T-shirt and a daily drink, plus a 20% discount on workshops; there is also a volunteer option. Deadline to submit a proposal is 16 August 2026. Enquiries: subscriptions@liveperformersmeeting.net.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: KAMP: Creative Body Institute Residency, Lesbos, Greece 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Creative Body Institute (CBI)

Location: Lesbos Island, Greece (eco-art hermitage in an olive grove on Mount Lepetymnos)

Award: Co-funded residency. Total cost per artist EUR 2,500; CBI funds EUR 1,350, so the artist contribution is EUR 1,150 (plus a EUR 100 non-refundable deposit deducted from that contribution). Does not include travel, visas, airport transfers, insurance or personal food. One full contribution waiver is available per cohort (request on the form; reviewed after selection, based on financial need and cohort balance).

Duration: Roughly 3-week residency per track; tracks run July 22 to September 26, 2026

Deadline: Closed 24 May 2026

KAMP is a communal residency from the artist-run non-profit Creative Body Institute, inviting queer-identifying artists to an ecological hermitage on Lesbos Island, Greece, to develop work exploring queer ecologies, queer temporality, and ecofeminist practices through a human and more-than-human, life-art ethos. CBI seeks artists whose practices engage embodiment, interdependence and temporality through performance, installation, sound, dance or experimental visual work. FOUR TRACKS: (1) Experimental Media and Performativity, Jul 22-Aug 10; (2) Relational and Site-Responsive Work, Aug 14-Sep 2; (3) Body-Based and Durational Practice, Sep 6-24/26; (4) Writer in Residence (dates coinciding with one of the above, for those focused on exegesis, a publication, or writing about prior embodied/artistic research). 5-7 artists are selected per cohort. Housing is simple cabin-based accommodation for relational living (Queer Family Cabin for 2-3, Cozy Cabin for solitude, Sanctuary Cabin for 2 sharing), with communal outdoor kitchen, shower house and bathrooms; this is an off-grid, sustainable environment (solar power, conscious water use, limited wifi). Artists must be comfortable with close communal living, shared rhythms, a collective food system (primarily vegetarian and pescatarian, sourced from local farmers), and unpaved terrain (which may be difficult for those with mobility impairments). INCLUDED: accommodation, shared indoor/outdoor workspaces, groceries for shared meals and staff support, two on-site CBI artist-faculty, mentorship and feedback, structured and informal group programming, work-in-progress sharing, and photo/video documentation. APPLICATION: online form (no draft saving) with project description (350 words) tied to the chosen track, 1-2 work samples, optional portfolio, artist bio(s) (350 words), and three short-answer questions; you may respond in any language. Apply individually (willing to share), as a duo, or as a trio/small group. Deadline May 24, 2026 (23:59 CET); virtual interviews May 25-29; final notifications June 1. Confirm within 5 days of acceptance with the EUR 100 deposit.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: workspacebrussels: Decoratelier Open Call 2026 (Spatial Research Residency)
item.description:
Organisation: workspacebrussels (residency at Decoratelier, Molenbeek)

Location: Decoratelier, Molenbeek, Brussels, Belgium

Award: EUR 5,000 budget (incl. VAT) for the residency, plus a 10x18m warehouse hall, wood and metal workshop access, reusable materials, junior-builder support three days a week, artistic and technical feedback, a possible internal showing, and weekday lunch. No application fee.

Duration: Three-week research residency (5 to 23 October 2026)

Deadline: Closed 31 May 2026

Research-focused residency at Decoratelier in Molenbeek, organised through workspacebrussels, for artists and collectives working with space, scenography, installation, architecture and performative environments. The residency prioritises research and material experimentation over production: applicants propose an investigation into spatial practice, ephemeral architecture or scenographic experimentation connected to the performing arts, engaging directly with the warehouse architecture and reusable materials. ELIGIBILITY: artists or collectives based in Belgium or abroad. NOTE: this is a context-specific research residency and does not provide scenographic support for existing productions. Apply via the workspacebrussels Decoratelier open call.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Tanztage Berlin Open Call 2027 (Sophiensaele)
item.description:
Organisation: Sophiensaele (Tanztage Berlin)

Location: Sophiensaele, Berlin, Germany

Award: Premieres receive EUR 10,000 to EUR 18,000 production support; revivals receive a EUR 5,000 flat fee. Both tracks also receive a EUR 410 evening fee per person per performance, rehearsal space, technical, curatorial, dramaturgical and PR support, and photo/video documentation. No application fee.

Duration: Festival 7-23 January 2027 (rehearsals from November 2026)

Deadline: 05 Jul 2026

Tanztage Berlin, the long-running platform at Sophiensaele for emerging choreographers and performance makers, is accepting applications for its 2027 edition (the final edition under artistic director Mateusz Szymanowka). The festival supports work across contemporary dance, choreography, installation, video and expanded performance formats, in two tracks: premieres (new productions) and revivals (completed works). ELIGIBILITY: emerging artists connected to Berlin (early in their careers, newly living in Berlin, or without prior Berlin project funding); a meaningful professional relationship to the city is required, though formal residency registration is not. The festival particularly welcomes projects involving marginalised perspectives, intergenerational casts and disabled artists. Apply via the Tanztage Berlin open-call page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Art Zoyd Studios: Electroacoustic & Electronic Music Residency 2026/2027
item.description:
Organisation: Art Zoyd Studios

Location: Valenciennes, France (in-person residency)

Award: Funded residency: accommodation and meals covered during the residency, travel reimbursed up to EUR 400, and support from a computer-music designer; a copyright bonus is possible. No artist fee or stipend. No application fee.

Duration: Residency across the 2026/2027 season

Deadline: Closed 29 May 2026

Production residency at Art Zoyd Studios in Valenciennes for composers, musicians and sound artists working in electroacoustic and electronic music, with technical support from a computer-music designer to develop new work. ELIGIBILITY: composers, musicians and artists practising in electroacoustic or electronic music; open worldwide. Apply via the Art Zoyd Studios residency request page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: AEIP: Art4Change Storytelling Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Africa-Europe Innovation Partnership (AEIP), implemented by The African Youth Café (TYC), funded by the European Union

Location: Hybrid (online training plus field engagement; applicants based in Africa, Europe or the diaspora)

Award: EUR 3,000 stipend per fellow to cover content production costs (transport, data, basic fieldwork needs), plus structured digital-storytelling training, editorial mentorship and platform access for publishing work. No application fee.

Duration: Six months (June to December 2026)

Deadline: Closed 29 May 2026

Hybrid digital storytelling fellowship from the Africa-Europe Innovation Partnership (AEIP), implemented by The African Youth Café and funded by the European Union, supporting young creatives to document and share stories of youth innovation across Africa, Europe and the diaspora through writing, photography, video, audio and digital storytelling. ELIGIBILITY: applicants aged 18-35 with an interest in storytelling, media or digital content creation, based in Africa, Europe or the diaspora, and able to commit to the full programme. IMPORTANT: as of May 2026 the African applicant quota has been reached - only European applicants are currently being accepted. FORMAT: online training sessions combined with field engagement for content production, plus continuous editorial mentorship and platform access for publishing. The canonical AEIP page does not list a fixed stipend amount, so confirm on application; the Terra Viva summary cites EUR 3,000. Apply via the Microsoft Forms application linked from the AEIP call page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Ars Electronica: State of the ART(ist) 2026 (Linz)
item.description:
Organisation: Ars Electronica, in cooperation with the Austrian Federal Ministry for European and International Affairs

Location: Linz, Austria (international open call; project presented at the Ars Electronica Festival)

Award: Main Prize EUR 6,000 plus Award of Distinction EUR 4,000. Both selected projects are presented at the Ars Electronica Festival (9 to 13 September 2026, Linz). No application fee.

Duration: One-off prize plus festival presentation

Deadline: Closed 24 May 2026

International open call by Ars Electronica for artists whose livelihoods and working conditions are severely affected by political repression, war, restrictions on freedom of expression, environmental crises, or economic and systemic exploitation. ELIGIBILITY: individual artists and collectives working in artistic, cultural, social, academic, activist or interdisciplinary fields; proof of risk or instability is a baseline eligibility requirement and projects are then evaluated on artistic quality.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Council of Europe: New Democratic Pact for Europe Democracy Hackathon 2026 (Strasbourg)
item.description:
Organisation: Council of Europe (under the Monaco Presidency of the Committee of Ministers), in partnership with No Hate Speech Week and Microsoft

Location: Palais de l'Europe, Strasbourg, France (hybrid: four online warm-up sessions leading to a three-day in-person finale)

Award: EUR 50,000 Microsoft-funded prize pool distributed among winning teams. Travel and accommodation covered for the 20 selected finalist teams. No application fee.

Duration: Online warm-up sessions plus three-day in-person hackathon (17-19 June 2026)

Deadline: 05 Jun 2026

Council of Europe civic-tech hackathon to combat online hate speech and develop tech solutions for democracy, run alongside No Hate Speech Week and backed by a Microsoft prize pool. Four challenge tracks cover privacy-preserving hate speech detection, identifying coordinated inauthentic behaviour, gender-based violence reporting systems, and innovative social transformation approaches. ELIGIBILITY: pre-formed multidisciplinary teams of 3-4 (typically a technologist/developer, a democracy or policy expert, and a designer or UX innovator); residents of the 46 Council of Europe member states; coding experience is not required for every team member. FORMAT: four online warm-up sessions, then a three-day in-person finale at the Palais de l'Europe, Strasbourg, 17-19 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Camargo Foundation: Fellowship 2027-2028
item.description:
Organisation: Fondation Camargo

Location: Cassis, France (Mediterranean coast)

Award: EUR 3,500 stipend (EUR 350/week x 10 weeks); basic transportation costs; housing in the foundation's apartments for the residency duration. No application fee.

Duration: 10 weeks in 2027 or 2028

Deadline: 01 Oct 2026

Fondation Camargo's 10-week residency for writers, scholars and artists on the Mediterranean coast in Cassis, France. About 14 fellows per cycle. Open to writers across genres (poetry, fiction, nonfiction, translation), as well as scholars and artists pursuing serious research, writing or creative projects in the humanities, social sciences and arts. ELIGIBILITY: international writers, scholars and artists; no nationality restriction.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Translation House Looren: Translators' Residencies and Pro Helvetia Stipends 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Uebersetzerhaus Looren

Location: Wernetshausen, Switzerland (Zurich region)

Award: Free residency stay (apartments at the translation house). Six competitive CHF 4,000 translation grants per year, each tied to a one-month residency. Pro Helvetia residency programme separately funded. James Joyce Scholarship and Looren Residency 2027 has its own conditions. No application fee.

Duration: Stays of typically 2 weeks to 2 months; CHF 4,000 stipend grants tied to one-month residencies

Deadline: 15 Oct 2026

Translators' residency at Uebersetzerhaus Looren in Wernetshausen near Zurich. Looren offers a free residency for literary translators with a current contract, including six competitive CHF 4,000 stipend grants per year tied to one-month residencies. The Pro Helvetia residency programme (separately funded) has a 2026-10-15 deadline for 2027 stays. The James Joyce Scholarship and Looren Residency 2027 closes 2026-10-31. General free residencies are accepted on a rolling basis throughout the year. ELIGIBILITY: literary translators with substantial published work and a current translation contract; any language combination is welcome. Apply via the application page on looren.net. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Mozilla Festival (MozFest) 2026: Call for Proposals - Wilding
item.description:
Organisation: Mozilla Foundation

Location: Barcelona, Spain (Recinte Fabra i Coats; co-hosted with the City of Barcelona as part of Open Tech Week)

Award: Limited travel support, not guaranteed: flights to Barcelona booked through Mozilla's platform; a local hotel room reserved by Mozilla; a 5-day Metro pass including airport transfer. No additional funds for food, other ground transport or travel insurance. Visa fee reimbursements handled through a separate process. NO artist fee, stipend or honorarium.

Duration: Three days, 28-30 October 2026

Deadline: Closed 24 May 2026

MozFest 2026 is Mozilla Foundation's annual festival of debates, labs and workshops, forums, showcases, talks and installations - this year at Recinte Fabra i Coats in Barcelona (28-30 October 2026), co-hosted with the City of Barcelona during Open Tech Week. 2026 THEME 'WILDING': a provocation about loosening rigid systems and creating conditions for communities and technologies to evolve collectively, naturally and intuitively. Inspired by ecological rewilding, it explores community-owned mesh networks, federated platforms, open protocols, community archives, and tools built on transparency, trust and mutual flourishing. Mozilla is inviting proposals that exemplify Wilding and celebrate community-led alternatives - work that is proof of what is possible. FESTIVAL TRACKS: Wilding Advocacy; Wilding Knowledge; Wilding Systems; Wilding With Nature; Youth Wilding; Wilding Creativity; Founders Wilding; Developers Wilding. PROCESS: submit a proposal via the MozFest CFP on the Mozilla Foundation festival site; travel support requests are reviewed after the CFP closes.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Passage Art-Residency-Programme 2026 (KOBRA Cultural Network)
item.description:
Organisation: Polygona Kunstverein e.V. (KOBRA Cultural Network)

Location: Wittenberg region, Saxony-Anhalt, Germany

Award: Per residency place: accommodation for the four-week stay; a grant of EUR 5,000; artists in residence with a child receive an additional EUR 1,000 toward temporary childcare. Travel, local transport, consumables and living expenses are paid by the artist; payment is made on receipt of an invoice.

Duration: Four weeks, October 2026

Deadline: Closed 30 May 2026

Two residency places in the Passage Art-Residency-Programme run by Polygona Kunstverein e.V. as part of the KOBRA Cultural Network, which explores continuities and discontinuities in the cultural landscape between Flaeming and Duebener Heide in the district of Wittenberg (Saxony-Anhalt, Germany). National and international artists working in photography and film are invited to apply. The two 2026 residencies run in October 2026 in collaboration with KOBRA partners Ferropolis Foundation for Industrial Heritage and the Research Library of Reformation Studies Wittenberg (RFB). TOPIC ONE (Ferropolis): artistic perspectives on the history of the former Zschornewitz coal-fired power station, the transformation of industrial landscapes, and visions for the future of post-industrial areas. TOPIC TWO (RFB): exploring the research library as a store of knowledge and examining the production of knowledge and its visual representation, drawing on a collection of around 230,000 volumes including Reformation-era first editions, manuscripts and historical writings. ELIGIBILITY: minimum age 18, no age limit; students are not eligible. Artists are selected by jury on the quality of submitted work; selected artists notified from 20 June 2026. FUNDING per place: accommodation for the four-week stay, a EUR 5,000 grant, and an additional EUR 1,000 for childcare if attending with a child. OBLIGATIONS: four weeks present in October 2026, realisation of an artistic project, provision of ten project photographs by 31 December 2026, delivery of a one-day workshop for young people, a public artist talk, and two days participating in filming for a documentary. Artists retain copyright and ownership of works created. Childcare and child-friendly accommodation can be arranged; partners may also accompany the artist at the artist's own additional cost. No application fee. Apply via the online form at passageair.org by 30 May 2026; applications accepted in German or English.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Magnetic 5: Franco-UK Artist Residencies (2026 call)
item.description:
Organisation: Fluxus Art Projects

Location: France and United Kingdom (artists from a French region apply to the associated UK residency; artists from a UK nation apply to the associated French region)

Award: Threshold monthly fee of £2,100 / €2,500, plus accommodation, access to a workspace, curatorial mentoring and networking opportunities.

Duration: Two to three months (residency dates set per host)

Deadline: 02 Jun 2026

Magnetic is a joint Franco-UK initiative coordinated by Fluxus Art Projects, bringing together ten institutions for a programme of 2-3 month artist residencies: five in France (Capc, Bordeaux; Frac Grand Large, Dunkirk; Frac Bretagne, Rennes; Villa Arson, Nice; Betonsalon, Paris) and five in the UK (Wysing Arts Centre and Gasworks in England; Flax Art Studios, Northern Ireland; Aberystwyth Arts Centre, Wales; Cove Park, Scotland). Artists from each of the five French regions apply for the associated UK residency; artists from each UK nation apply for the associated French region. Societal and environmental issues are at the heart of the programme; the residencies are situated, with selected artists expected to work in resonance with each host's geographical, historical, societal and artistic context.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Weaving Liberation: Digital Justice Fund (Pilot Round 2026)
item.description:
Organisation: Weaving Liberation (resourced by Ford Foundation, Luminate, and Oak Foundation)

Location: Europe (Council of Europe member states; at least 50% of the group must be based in one or multiple Council of Europe countries)

Award: Grants between €10,000 and €50,000 per group, to be used over up to two years (2026-2028). Total Fund budget for this first round: €500,000.

Duration: Up to two years of project work, between 2026 and 2028

Deadline: Closed 24 May 2026

The Digital Justice Fund works towards nurturing an ecosystem where groups resisting violent technological harms and imagining more nourishing relationships with technologies have the resources they need to dream, organise, build and thrive towards liberatory digital presents and futures. We are interested in funding: Community building and sustaining around digital justice issues: community organising, peer-to-peer support, relationship-building activities, etc. Knowledge and capacity building: popular and political education, investigative journalism, skills exchanges, issue mappings (e.g., technological harms, community needs and desires), advancing liberatory narratives around technology, artistic interventions, and other creative approaches, etc. Coalition and movement building: advocacy, strategic litigation, campaigning, etc. Tech-related mutual aid: tech repair circles, peer support on digital safety and security, shared technical infrastructures for movement work, software/hardware sharing initiatives, etc. Life-affirming technology work: prototyping, piloting, or expanding community-centred, transfeminist, anti-colonial technologies, etc. Key dates: platform registration deadline Sunday 24 May 2026, 23:59 CET (registration is required by this date even if application is not yet complete); final application deadline Sunday 21 June 2026, 23:59 CET.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: BrightBrain: Artist Residency 2026 (TU Braunschweig Science & Art Lab)
item.description:
Organisation: Science and Art Lab at TU Braunschweig, in cooperation with the BrightBrain research consortium

Location: Braunschweig, Germany (in-person residency at TU Braunschweig)

Award: Artist fee €2,400 per month (gross), plus travel costs and accommodation covered, plus production budget up to €3,500 (gross).

Duration: Autumn/winter 2026 residency (start from September 2026, exact start date flexible). Length agreed with host.

Deadline: Closed 25 May 2026

Open call for an international artist-in-residence at the Science and Art Lab at TU Braunschweig, in cooperation with the BrightBrain research consortium, as part of the Ecoversity initiative. The residency aims to connect artistic and scientific perspectives. Artists are invited to propose their own research-based projects related to microphotonics, biomedicine, technology, neurodegenerative diseases, neurobiology, neuromorphic computing, and microelectronics (the current resident is collaborating with the Nitride Technology Center on light transmission in digital applications including micro-LEDs and neuromorphic computing). Strong fit for art-science, sensory translation, critical-data, and neuroscience-informed practices. Eligibility: international artists; should show interest in biomedicine, technology and innovation in their artistic work; be comfortable working in interdisciplinary environments; be motivated to collaborate with experts from science, culture and education; be willing to participate in two public events during the residency; and be able to communicate in English. Application by PDF to artist-residency@tu-braunschweig.de with subject 'BrightBrain residency' (if files exceed 25 MB, upload via the TU Braunschweig Nextcloud link in the call). Application deadline 25 May 2026. Jury decision expected end of June 2026; residency starts from September 2026 (exact date flexible). Free to apply.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: WIELS: Residency Programme 2027 (Brussels)
item.description:
Organisation: WIELS

Location: Brussels, Belgium (in-person residency at WIELS contemporary art centre)

Award: Artistic recognition grant of €3,000 per selected artist (4 artists selected). Awarded as an artistic distinction without any production obligation or service in return. Under Belgian tax law, artistic prizes may be treated as miscellaneous income and may benefit from a partial tax exemption within certain limits (depends on the beneficiary's individual situation).

Duration: Six months in 2027, choose one block: January-June 2027 OR July-December 2027

Deadline: Closed 31 May 2026

Annual residency programme at WIELS (Brussels-based centre for contemporary art). This call is open EXCLUSIVELY to Belgian artists or artists who have been living and working in Belgium for at least three years, and who have contributed to the Brussels contemporary art scene during that period. An independent jury selects four artists, each awarded a €3,000 artistic recognition grant. The grant is a distinction supporting the quality of the artistic practice; there is no production requirement or contractual service obligation attached. Residency period is six months in 2027 (January-June or July-December block, applicant choice). Application deadline 31 May 2026. Application materials via online form: personal information; artistic approach (max 250 words); statement of intent describing projects, ideas and expectations for the residency (max 250 words); three professional references (name, email, phone, profession); and portfolio + CV via Option 1 (Dropbox/Google Drive link with up to 10 images/videos plus a numbered descriptive list with title, date, technique, dimensions and ~50-word description per item, plus CV) or Option 2 (PDF with up to 10 captioned images plus CV). Videos may also be hosted on YouTube/Vimeo (with password if needed); videos of still works are not accepted. All share links must be set to 'anyone with the link can view'; links requiring access requests will not be considered. Questions: residency@wiels.org (do not email about results, notifications will be sent). Free to apply. Note: this is a Belgium-restricted call and is not open to artists without a Belgian / 3+ years-in-Belgium connection.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Ohme x EXPERIENCE: Open Call - Experience Lab (Digital Artist Residency on Consciousness)
item.description:
Organisation: Ohme (in collaboration with EXPERIENCE / CRCN, Université libre de Bruxelles)

Location: Brussels, Belgium (in-person residency at Ohme's premises, USquare, Brussels)

Award: Artist fee: €5,300 (approx. 20 working days based on CP 304 salary scales, max 10 years experience). Production budget up to €2,000. Plus professional documentation, access to EXPERIENCE datasets/tools/documentation, preparatory reading list, Ohme team support, and workspace at USquare.

Duration: 20-day engagement spread between September and November 2026, with public restitution in December 2026. Preparatory period July-August 2026 (reading list and onboarding materials).

Deadline: Closed 31 May 2026

Open call from Ohme (Brussels-based research and curatorial platform at the intersection of art and science) for a digital visual artist or collective to develop an artistic project in residence with EXPERIENCE, an ERC-funded research programme led by Prof. Axel Cleeremans at the Centre for Research in Cognition and Neurosciences (CRCN), Université libre de Bruxelles. EXPERIENCE investigates the nature of subjective experience (phenomenology) and builds 3D maps of 'phenomenal space' from large datasets of similarity and preference judgments. The selected artist works directly with these empirical datasets (similarity judgments, subjective preference data describing how people perceive, compare and value conscious experience) as primary creative material, not as illustration. Possible directions include information design that critically engages with visualising consciousness data; generative or algorithmic works using subjective valuation datasets as compositional grammar; data visualisation as artistic practice; creative coding exploring neural/latent space and perception/emotion/subjectivity; and critical or poetic approaches to measuring/mapping inner experience. Eligibility: visual artist or collective connected to the contemporary art field of the Fédération Wallonie-Bruxelles (live, work, or have a professional practice within the French-speaking art community of Belgium); established practice in digital art, new media, computational art, data visualisation or information design used as artistic practice; experience working with data as creative material (e.g. Processing, p5.js, openFrameworks, TouchDesigner, Max/MSP, or equivalent); critical engagement with the tools/systems behind information production and dissemination; interest in mind/perception/cognition/neuroscience; available in person in Brussels September-November 2026 and for the December restitution; English proficiency (working language); French and/or Dutch appreciated. Ohme is NOT looking for artists who already work on consciousness or neuroscience, but practitioners whose existing methods could be mobilised in this scientific context. Sample EXPERIENCE datasets: https://osf.io/suqkp/overview?view_only=fdc73f1485664a2c9c7571e0c62abe6a (paper: 'Exploring the role of micro-valence in the phenomenal space', https://doi.org/10.1093/nc/niag005). Selection by interdisciplinary jury of EXPERIENCE and Ohme representatives; interviews with top three candidates by phone/video/in-person in late June 2026; selected artist announced late June 2026. Application: single PDF in English to opencall@ohme.be (subject: 'Application Experience Lab') by 31 May 2026, 23:59 CET, including up-to-date CV (English), portfolio (English), 1-page motivation text (English) on interest in the residency and consciousness theme plus technical competences, and the completed contact form (downloadable from Ohme's site). Free to apply.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
